{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5575","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5575/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5575/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5575/events","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5575","id":2718301995,"node_id":"I_kwDOCpMuHc6iBf8r","number":5575,"title":"Read from file to an allocatable array of strings fails","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":1287342341,"node_id":"MDU6TGFiZWwxMjg3MzQyMzQx","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/labels/bug","name":"bug","color":"d73a4a","default":true,"description":"Something isn't working"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":2,"created_at":"2024-12-04T16:49:56Z","updated_at":"2024-12-04T22:46:52Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"MRE:
```fortran
program file_read
implicit none
integer :: rows, cols, i
character(len=:), allocatable :: lines(:)
integer :: u
open(newunit=u, file="input.txt", status='old')
rows = 2
cols = 2
print *, rows, cols
allocate(character(len=cols) :: lines(rows))
do i = 1, rows
    print *, i
    read(u, '(A)') lines(i) ! fails here
    print *, lines(i)
end do
close(u)
end program
```
Here is the `input.txt`:
```
xx
yy
```
In GFortran:
```console
$ gfortran a.f90 && ./a.out 
           2           2
           1
 xx
           2
 yy
```
And LFortran:
```console
$ lfortran a.f90 
2    2
1
```

The following diff makes it work:
```diff
--- a/a.f90
+++ b/a.f90
@@ -2,6 +2,7 @@ program file_read
 implicit none
 integer :: rows, cols, i
 character(len=:), allocatable :: lines(:)
+character(len=100) :: tmp
 integer :: u
 open(newunit=u, file="input.txt", status='old')
 rows = 2
@@ -10,7 +11,8 @@ print *, rows, cols
 allocate(character(len=cols) :: lines(rows))
 do i = 1, rows
     print *, i
-    read(u, '(A)') lines(i) ! fails here
+    read(u, '(A)') tmp ! fails here
+    lines(i) = tmp
     print *, lines(i)
 end do
 close(u)
```
Then:
```console
$ gfortran a.f90 && ./a.out
           2           2
           1
 xx
           2
 yy
$ lfortran a.f90           
2    2
1
xx
2
yy
```

So the bug is just reading into an allocatable array of strings. Reading into just one string and then assigning to the allocatable array works.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5575/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5575/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2518713005","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5575#issuecomment-2518713005","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/5575","id":2518713005,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6WIIKt","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-04T22:46:51Z","updated_at":"2024-12-04T22:46:51Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"For the allocatable version, this line should allocate all the strings:
```
 allocate(character(len=cols) :: lines(rows))
```


```fortran 
module gg
    contains
    function func_arr3(inp_ref) result(ret_arr)
        integer, intent(in) :: inp_ref
        integer,allocatable :: ret_arr(:)
        ! print *, allocated(ret_arr)
        allocate(ret_arr(1))
        ret_arr = [inp_ref]
        print *, "worked", inp_ref
    end function
end module


program p
    use gg
    implicit none
    integer, allocatable :: inp(:)
    integer :: i =77
    
    do i= 1, 3
        inp = func_arr3(i)
    end do 
end program  
end program

Minimal Reproducible Example

```f90
program main
    character(len=20) :: textblock
    textblock = '&!'
end program main
```

On compiling the above program with LFortran, it raises an error:
```zsh
~/Doc/lfortran main !1 ?4 ❯ main_lfortran test.f90 --show-ast          lf 10:36:53 PM
tokenizer error: Token ''' is not recognized
 --> test1.f90:3:17
  |
3 |     textblock = '&!'
  |                 ^ token not recognized


Note: Please report unclear, confusing or incorrect messages as bugs at
https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues.

types","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"labels":[{"id":4362097645,"node_id":"LA_kwDOCpMuHc8AAAABBABT7Q","url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/labels/asr","name":"asr","color":"52F0F7","default":false,"description":"ASR Related changes"}],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":7,"created_at":"2024-04-02T17:23:53Z","updated_at":"2024-12-05T19:23:43Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"We have "primitive" types like Integer, Real, Complex.

And non-primitive types: Array, List, Dict, Set, String, Class, Struct

The non-primitive types require some kind of deallocation or freeing, even if they are not "allocatable".

The Allocatable type's semantic is that the entity is not even allocated, and one must call "Allocate" to create it. Non-allocatable types are always created (whether on stack or a heap, that's an implementation detail).

However, non-primitive types have to be freed if the backend chooses to implement them on a heap.

The logic of freeing is complicated (many ways to escape from a block), so it should be explicit in ASR and checked by ASR verify.

Another idea: we can have a general "OutOfScope" ASR node that will list all variables that go out of scope at this point, including real and integer. The backend will then choose which of these variables should be freed and how. For example this design would allow for the backend to add some more complicated structure to even primitive types in Debug mode, perhaps to keep track of some statistics, or if it is a "target", then pointers can point to it, then it can keep track of who points to it, and when it goes out of scope, to set all pointers to "dangling". It needs to know when it goes out of scope. So I think having a general OutOfScope node that is called for *all* variables might be the way to go.

It seems that Allocatable type (primitive or non-primitive) has exactly the same handling as a non-allocatable non-primitive type, except that there must be an explicit allocation and the deallocation must check at runtime if the type was allocated.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3770/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3770/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2521220552","html_url":"https://github.com/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3770#issuecomment-2521220552","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/3770","id":2521220552,"node_id":"IC_kwDOCpMuHc6WRsXI","user":{"login":"certik","id":20568,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNTY4","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20568?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik","html_url":"https://github.com/certik","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/certik/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-12-05T19:23:42Z","updated_at":"2024-12-05T19:23:42Z","author_association":"CONTRIBUTOR","body":"We discussed the pros and cons on November 14. We decided as follows:

* For now we'll do *all* finalization using a pass that will be executed right before the LLVM (and other) backends
* The ASR semantics will be that deallocation happens automatically. So AST->ASR nor ASR passes will insert any such nodes. That seems consistent. (In the other design we would insert deallocations in some places, but not all, for example it is hard to insert deallocation inside expressions, so the other design would not be fully consistent in this respect.)
* We will get to beta this way; that will allow us to see and figure out all the corner cases with deallocation and we will be doing it at one place in the pass, so it should be maintainable
* This still leaves the door open to later decide if we want to do the other design, and if so, we will know what exactly is involved and we can effectively merge the pass with any other ASR pass that needs it, and enforce it with ASR verify; The advantage of the other design is that there will be less number of passes (so possibly faster compilation which we care about a lot), but possibly the logic will be scattered over many passes, so maybe not worth doing; but the door is open, so we will revisit once we understand all the requirements after beta.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/lfortran/lfortran/issues/comments/2521220552/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
